extra.reasoning: The property requires that in every reachable state, opted_out=true implies sent=false (no send after opt-out). Transition t5 ([t5] on send_attempt if (opted_out and ticks>=5): sent := true) directly sets sent:=true while opted_out is true, which violates the property. A reachable witness: starting from the initial state (opted_out=false, sent=false, ticks=0), fire optout (t1: opted_out=true, sent=false, ticks=0), then five tick events (t4 each time, raising ticks to 5 and keeping sent=false), then send_attempt. At the send_attempt, opted_out=true and ticks>=5, so t5's guard holds and it sets sent:=true, producing the state opted_out=true, sent=true. (Note t3 also matches send_attempt under opt-out and would keep sent=false, but the guards of t3 and t5 overlap when ticks>=5, so an execution choosing t5 is reachable; under a most-specific/last-rule priority t5 would even fire deterministically.) The 7-event sequence is within the 9-event bound. Verified by simulation. Therefore the property is violated.
